reasoning:

In Nicaragua, the authoritarian regime of Daniel Ortega and Rosario Murillo has established a pervasive police state, violently suppressing civil society, closing universities, and arresting all genuine political opposition since the 2018 protests. For the past year, 'Riots' as classified by ACLED have essentially been at zero in Nicaragua (with the calculated baseline threshold near 1.0). For this question to resolve positive, there would need to be 11 or more distinct ACLED 'Riot' events in the 30-day windows leading up to the resolution dates. 

As of the knowledge cutoff (Nov 9, 2025), there are no ongoing protests or riots, neutralizing the risk for the 2025-11-16 resolution date (which includes mostly retrospective knowledge). Looking forward to December 2025 and February 2026, while the 2026 general elections could provide an eventual catalyst, the intense repression makes organizing a mass movement that breaks out into 11+ distinct violent riots in a single month highly unlikely over this short horizon. Consequently, my probabilities are set to the minimum allowable bound of 0.05 for the nearest date, slowly increasing over time to reflect marginally higher structural uncertainty for February 2026.
